PR surface: Tests +110, Docs +88. Total +198 across 4 files.

target.title: Document snapshot restore proof
target.body:
## Summary

This is PR 4 in the snapshot stack. It proves and documents the operator-facing restore workflow for snapshot artifacts.

Adds:
- a source-level workflow proof that snapshots a named OpenClaw database target, copies only the snapshot artifact directory, and restores from that copied artifact
- assertions that the syncable artifact directory contains `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`, not live SQLite `-wal` / `-shm` files
- a `docs/cli/snapshot.md` CLI reference page explaining what to sync, what not to sync, named targets, and restore behavior
- a CLI index link and command-tree entry for `openclaw snapshot`

## Why

The important operational distinction from #94675 is not "copy SQLite files more carefully." It is: OpenClaw should produce a safe sync artifact for SQLite state, and hosts should sync that artifact instead of the hot runtime file family.

This PR makes that boundary explicit:

- live runtime files remain local SQLite state
- snapshot directories are the portable/syncable artifact
- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact

## Stack

- Depends on #94799
- Depends on #94717
- Builds on #94694
- RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20
- Related: #94675, #94646

## Scope

This does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, or restore-on-boot behavior. Those remain future phases. This PR only proves the artifact/restore boundary and documents operator usage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw's snapshot workflow now has an explicit proof that a named SQLite target can be converted into a syncable artifact directory, copied separately from live SQLite files, and restored into a verified fresh SQLite database.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, source test harness with temporary SQLite databases, WAL mode enabled, copied snapshot directories, and local restore targets.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
2. `node_modules\.bin\oxlint src\commands\snapshot.test.ts`
3. `npx prettier --check docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/index.md`
4. `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:
Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests). `oxlint`, Prettier check, and `git diff --check` completed cleanly.

Observed result after fix:
The new workflow test creates a WAL-mode `--target global` source database, snapshots it, verifies the snapshot directory contains only `database.sqlite` and `manifest.json`, copies that directory to a separate synced location, restores from the copied artifact, removes stale restore-side sidecars, passes SQLite integrity check, and reads the expected restored row.

What was not tested:
No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR.
